1	# MCO Domain Model - CRDs
2	
3	All CRDs belong to API group `machineconfiguration.openshift.io/v1`. Types are defined in `vendor/github.com/openshift/api/machineconfiguration/v1/`. Compatibility Level 1 (stable within major release, min 12 months).
4	
5	## MachineConfig
6	
7	Cluster-scoped Ignition config fragment. No status subresource.
8	
9	**Spec:** `config` (Ignition RawExtension), `kernelArguments` ([]string), `extensions` ([]string), `fips` (bool), `kernelType` (default|realtime|64k-pages), `osImageURL`, `baseOSExtensionsContainerImage`.
10	
11	Multiple MachineConfigs are merged by the Render Controller into a single rendered config per pool. Merge order is deterministic (sorted by name). Conflicts in file paths use last-writer-wins.
12	
13	## MachineConfigPool (short: mcp)
14	
15	Groups nodes via `nodeSelector` and selects MachineConfigs via `machineConfigSelector`. Tracks update rollout status.
16	
17	**Key spec:** `paused` (bool), `maxUnavailable` (IntOrString), `pinnedImageSets` (max 100), `configuration` (target rendered config).
18	
19	**Key status:** `machineCount`, `updatedMachineCount`, `readyMachineCount`, `degradedMachineCount`, `conditions` (Updated, Updating, Degraded, NodeDegraded, RenderDegraded, Building, BuildSuccess, BuildFailed, etc.).
20	
21	**Validation rules:** machineCount >= updatedMachineCount, machineCount >= readyMachineCount, etc.
22	
23	Default pools: `master`, `worker`. Custom pools inherit from worker via `machineConfigSelector`.
24	
25	## ControllerConfig
26	
27	Cluster-wide configuration consumed by Template Controller to generate base MachineConfigs.
28	
29	**Key spec:** `clusterDNSIP`, `rootCAData`, `kubeAPIServerServingCAData`, `additionalTrustBundle`, `pullSecret`, `images` (map), `baseOSContainerImage`, `releaseImage`, `proxy`, `infra`, `dns`, `ipFamilies` (IPv4|IPv6|DualStack|DualStackIPv6Primary), `networkType`.
30	
31	**Status conditions:** TemplateControllerRunning, TemplateControllerCompleted, TemplateControllerFailing.
32	
33	## KubeletConfig
34	
35	Applies upstream kubelet configuration overrides per pool.
36	
37	**Key spec:** `machineConfigPoolSelector` (LabelSelector), `kubeletConfig` (RawExtension - upstream KubeletConfiguration), `autoSizingReserved` (bool), `logLevel` (0-10), `tlsSecurityProfile`.
38	
39	Controller generates a MachineConfig with the merged kubelet config and drops file at `/etc/kubernetes/kubelet.conf`.
40	
41	## ContainerRuntimeConfig (short: ctrcfg)
42	
43	Tunes CRI-O and container runtime settings per pool.
44	
45	**Key spec:** `machineConfigPoolSelector`, `containerRuntimeConfig` with fields: `pidsLimit`, `logLevel` (fatal..debug), `logSizeMax` (>= 8192), `overlaySize` (default 10GB), `defaultRuntime` (crun|runc), `additionalLayerStores` (max 5), `additionalImageStores` (max 10), `additionalArtifactStores` (max 10).
46	
47	Store paths validated: absolute, 1-256 chars, pattern `^/[a-zA-Z0-9/._-]+$`, no consecutive slashes.
48	
49	## MachineConfigNode
50	
51	Per-node update tracking. Created/managed by Node Controller and updated by MCD.
52	
53	**Spec:** `configVersion.desired` (target MC ref), `pool.name`, `node.name`.
54	
55	**Status conditions:** Updated, UpdatePrepared, UpdateExecuted, UpdatePostActionComplete, UpdateComplete, Resumed, UpdateCompatible, AppliedFiles, AppliedFilesAndOS, Cordoned, Uncordoned, Drained, RebootedNode, ReloadedCRIO, PinnedImageSetsDegraded, PinnedImageSetsProgressing.
56	
57	## MachineOSConfig
58	
59	Defines on-cluster layering (OCL) build inputs for a pool.
60	
61	**Key spec:** `machineConfigPool` (ref), `buildInputs`, `containerFile` ([]ContainerFile with `content` and optional `containerfileArch`).
62	
63	## MachineOSBuild
64	
65	Tracks execution of an OCL build job.
66	
67	**Key spec:** `machineOSConfig` (ref), `machineConfig` (ref).
68	
69	**Key status:** `digestedImagePushSpec` (built image), `builder.job` (Job ref with name/namespace).
70	
71	## OSImageStream
72	
73	OS image references for the release. Managed by the osimagestream binary.
74	
75	**Status:** `defaultStream`, `availableStreams` ([]OSImageStreamSpec with `name`, `osImage`, `osExtensionsImage`).
76	
77	## PinnedImageSet
78	
79	Pre-pulls container images to nodes in matching pools.
80	
81	**Spec:** `machineConfigPoolSelector` (LabelSelector), `pinnedImages` ([]PinnedImage with `name`).
82	
83	## Feature-Gated CRD Variants
84	
85	CRDs ship in multiple variants for feature gates: Default, DevPreviewNoUpgrade, CustomNoUpgrade, TechPreviewNoUpgrade, OKD, Hypershift, SelfManagedHA. Located in `vendor/github.com/openshift/api/machineconfiguration/v1/zz_generated.crd-manifests/`.
86

159	func (ctrl *OSBuildController) shutdownController() {
160		// Time box the controller shutdown delay process to ten seconds. This is
161		// approximately how long it takes the kubelet to send a SIGKILL to a
162		// process that it has already sent a SIGTERM to.
163		shutdownCtx, shutdownCancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), ctrl.config.MaxShutdownDelay)
164		defer shutdownCancel()
165	
166		klog.Infof("Determining if a shutdown delay up to %s is required", ctrl.config.MaxShutdownDelay)
167	
168		if err := ctrl.shutdownDelayHandler.handleShutdown(shutdownCtx, ctrl.config.ShutdownPollInterval); err != nil {
169			klog.Warningf("Error occurred during graceful shutdown: %s. Some objects may be orphaned as a result.", err)
170		}
171	
172		// Shut down the work queue.
173		ctrl.execQueue.ShutDown()
174	
175		// Handle any crashes.
176		utilruntime.HandleCrash()
177	
178		klog.Infof("OSBuildController has shut down")
179		close(ctrl.shutdownChan)
180	}
181	
182	// Returns a channel that can be used to determine whether the controller
183	// shutdown is complete.
184	func (ctrl *OSBuildController) ShutdownChan() <-chan struct{} {
185		return ctrl.shutdownChan
186	}

188	func (ctrl *OSBuildController) Run(parentCtx context.Context, workers int) {
189		klog.Infof("Starting OSBuildController")
190	
191		// The main controller context. We purposely do not inherit from the parent
192		// context because we need to control the shutdown process.
193		ctrlCtx, ctrlCancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
194		defer func() {
195			klog.Infof("Shutting down OSBuildController")
196			ctrl.shutdownController()
197			ctrlCancel()
198		}()
199	
200		ctrl.ctx = ctrlCtx
201	
202		ctrl.informers.start(ctrlCtx)
203	
204		if !cache.WaitForCacheSync(ctrlCtx.Done(), ctrl.hasSynced...) {
205			return
206		}
207	
208		ctrl.execQueue.Start(ctrlCtx, workers)
209	
210		<-parentCtx.Done()
211	}

213	type kubeObject interface {
214		k8sruntime.Object
215		GetName() string
216	}
217	
218	type enqueuedFuncInfo struct {
219		kubeObject kubeObject
220		funcName   string
221	}
222	
223	func (e *enqueuedFuncInfo) getFuncName() string {
224		split := strings.Split(e.funcName, ".")
225		splitLen := len(split)
226		return strings.Join(split[splitLen-2:splitLen], ".")
227	}
228	
229	func (e *enqueuedFuncInfo) String() string {
230		kind, err := utils.GetKindForObject(e.kubeObject)
231		if err != nil && kind == "" {
232			kind = "<unknown object kind>"
233		}
234	
235		name := e.kubeObject.GetName()
236	
237		if e.funcName == "" {
238			return fmt.Sprintf("<kind: %q, name: %q>", kind, name)
239		}
240	
241		return fmt.Sprintf("<kind: %q, name: %q, func: %q>", kind, name, e.getFuncName())
242	}
243	
244	func (ctrl *OSBuildController) enqueueFuncForObject(obj kubeObject, toRun func(context.Context) error) {
245		info := &enqueuedFuncInfo{
246			kubeObject: obj,
247		}
248	
249		// Add the callers name to the id so that if / when something is dropped out
250		// of the queue, it's more obvious what was happening at the time.
251		pc, _, _, ok := runtime.Caller(1)
252		details := runtime.FuncForPC(pc)
253		if ok && details != nil {
254			info.funcName = details.Name()
255		}
256	
257		ctrl.execQueue.EnqueueAfterWithNameAndContext(ctrl.ctx, info.String(), ctrl.config.UpdateDelay, func(ctx context.Context) error {
258			cctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)
259			defer cancel()
260	
261			err := toRun(cctx)
262			if err == nil {
263				return nil
264			}
265	
266			// If the parent context is canceled, we cannot retry the current
267			// operation. Including ErrDropFromQueue is probably unnecessary, but doing
268			// so should be fine.
269			if ctrl.ctx.Err() != nil {
270				klog.Warning("Parent context canceled, additional retries will not be attempted")
271				return errors.Join(err, ctrlcommon.ErrDropFromQueue)
272			}
273	
274			return err
275		})
276	}

279		mosb := cur.(*mcfgv1.MachineOSBuild)

281			return ctrl.buildReconciler.AddMachineOSBuild(ctx, mosb)
282		})
283	}

107	// Executes whenever a MachineOSConfig is updated. If the build inputs have
108	// changed, a new MachineOSBuild should be created.
109	func (b *buildReconciler) updateMachineOSConfig(ctx context.Context, old, cur *mcfgv1.MachineOSConfig) error {
110		// If we have gained the rebuild annotation, we should delete the current MachineOSBuild associated with this MachineOSConfig.
111		if !hasRebuildAnnotation(old) && hasRebuildAnnotation(cur) {
112			if err := b.rebuildMachineOSConfig(ctx, cur); err != nil {
113				return fmt.Errorf("could not rebuild MachineOSConfig %q: %w", cur.Name, err)
114			}
115	
116			return nil
117		}
118	
119		// Whenever the MachineOSConfig spec has changed, create a new MachineOSBuild.
120		if !equality.Semantic.DeepEqual(old.Spec, cur.Spec) {
121			klog.Infof("Detected MachineOSConfig change for %s", cur.Name)
122			return b.createNewMachineOSBuildOrReuseExisting(ctx, cur, false)
123		}
124	
125		return b.syncMachineOSConfigs(ctx)
126	}

128	// Rebuilds the most current build associated with a MachineOSConfig whenever
129	// the rebuild annotation is applied. This is done by deleting the current
130	// MachineOSBuild and allowing the controller to replace it with a new one.
131	func (b *buildReconciler) rebuildMachineOSConfig(ctx context.Context, mosc *mcfgv1.MachineOSConfig) error {
132		klog.Infof("MachineOSConfig %q has rebuild annotation (%q)", mosc.Name, constants.RebuildMachineOSConfigAnnotationKey)
133	
134		if !hasCurrentBuildAnnotation(mosc) {
135			klog.Infof("MachineOSConfig %q does not have current build annotation (%q) set, skipping rebuild", mosc.Name, constants.CurrentMachineOSBuildAnnotationKey)
136			return nil
137		}
138	
139		mosbName := mosc.Annotations[constants.CurrentMachineOSBuildAnnotationKey]
140	
141		mosb, err := b.machineOSBuildLister.Get(mosbName)
142		if err != nil {
143			return ignoreErrIsNotFound(fmt.Errorf("cannot rebuild MachineOSConfig %q: %w", mosc.Name, err))
144		}
145	
146		if err := b.deleteMachineOSBuild(ctx, mosb); err != nil {
147			return fmt.Errorf("could not delete MachineOSBuild %q for MachineOSConfig %q: %w", mosb.Name, mosc.Name, err)
148		}
149	
150		if err := b.createNewMachineOSBuildOrReuseExisting(ctx, mosc, true); err != nil {
151			return fmt.Errorf("could not create new MachineOSBuild for MachineOSConfig %q: %w", mosc.Name, err)
152		}
153	
154		klog.Infof("MachineOSConfig %q is now rebuilding", mosc.Name)
155	
156		return nil
157	}

159	// Runs whenever a new MachineOSConfig is added. Determines if a new
160	// MachineOSBuild should be created and then creates it, if needed.
161	func (b *buildReconciler) addMachineOSConfig(ctx context.Context, mosc *mcfgv1.MachineOSConfig) error {
162		// If the rebuild annotation is present (e.g., pod restarted while a rebuild
163		// was pending), process it now instead of falling through to the normal sync
164		// path which would see the existing MOSB and return early.
165		if hasRebuildAnnotation(mosc) {
166			return b.rebuildMachineOSConfig(ctx, mosc)
167		}
168	
169		// Check for pre-built image seeding annotation - only seed if not already seeded
170		if preBuiltImage, hasImage := getPreBuiltImage(mosc); hasImage {
171			if shouldSeedWithPreBuiltImage(mosc) {
172				klog.Infof("MachineOSConfig %q has pre-built image annotation, attempting to seed with image %q", mosc.Name, preBuiltImage)
173				return b.seedMachineOSConfigWithExistingImage(ctx, mosc, preBuiltImage)
174			}
175			// If we get here, either already seeded or has current build - proceed normally
176			klog.V(4).Infof("MachineOSConfig %q has pre-built image annotation but seeding already complete, proceeding with normal processing", mosc.Name)
177		}
178	
179		// Existing logic for normal MachineOSConfig processing
180		return b.syncMachineOSConfig(ctx, mosc)
181	}

191	// Performs the deletion reconciliation of the MachineOSConfig.
192	func (b *buildReconciler) deleteMachineOSConfig(ctx context.Context, mosc *mcfgv1.MachineOSConfig) error {
193		klog.Infof("Removing MachineOSBuild(s) associated with non-existent MachineOSConfig %s", mosc.Name)
194	
195		mosbList, err := b.machineOSBuildLister.List(utils.MachineOSBuildForPoolSelector(mosc))
196		if err != nil {
197			return fmt.Errorf("could not list MachineOSBuilds for MachineOSConfig %s: %w", mosc.Name, err)
198		}
199	
200		for _, mosb := range mosbList {
201			// Only delete MOSBs owned by this specific MOSC instance. A new MOSC
202			// with the same name (but different UID) may already exist and have
203			// created new MOSBs that match the same label selector.
204			if !metav1.IsControlledBy(mosb, mosc) {
205				klog.Infof("Skipping MachineOSBuild %s: not owned by deleted MachineOSConfig %s (UID %s)", mosb.Name, mosc.Name, mosc.UID)
206				continue
207			}
208			if err := b.deleteMachineOSBuild(ctx, mosb); err != nil {
209				return fmt.Errorf("could not delete MachineOSBuild %s for MachineOSConfig %s: %w", mosb.Name, mosc.Name, err)
210			}
211		}
212	
213		return nil
214	}

271	// Performs the reconciliation whenever the MachineOSBuild is updated, such as
272	// cleaning up the build artifacts upon success.
273	func (b *buildReconciler) updateMachineOSBuild(ctx context.Context, old, current *mcfgv1.MachineOSBuild) error {
274		mosc, err := utils.GetMachineOSConfigForMachineOSBuild(current, b.utilListers())
275		if err != nil {
276			// If a MachineOSConfig is deleted before the MachineOSBuild is, we should
277			// ignore any not found errors.
278			return ignoreErrIsNotFound(fmt.Errorf("could not update MachineOSBuild %q: %w", current.Name, err))
279		}
280	
281		oldState := ctrlcommon.NewMachineOSBuildState(old)
282		curState := ctrlcommon.NewMachineOSBuildState(current)
283	
284		if !oldState.HasBuildConditions() && curState.HasBuildConditions() &&
285			!oldState.IsInInitialState() && curState.IsInInitialState() {
286			klog.Infof("Initial MachineOSBuild %q status update", current.Name)
287			return nil
288		}
289	
290		if !oldState.IsBuildFailure() && curState.IsBuildFailure() {
291			klog.Infof("MachineOSBuild %s failed, leaving ephemeral objects in place for inspection", current.Name)
292	
293			mcp, err := b.machineConfigPoolLister.Get(mosc.Spec.MachineConfigPool.Name)
294			if err != nil {
295				return fmt.Errorf("could not get MachineConfigPool from MachineOSConfig %q: %w", mosc.Name, err)
296			}
297	
298			// Always update ImageBuildDegraded condition based on current active build status
299			return b.updateImageBuildDegradedCondition(ctx, mcp, mosc)
300		}
301	
302		// If the build was successful, clean up the build objects and propagate the
303		// final image pushspec onto the MachineOSConfig object.
304		// Also update BuildDegraded condition based on current active build status
305		if !oldState.IsBuildSuccess() && curState.IsBuildSuccess() {
306			klog.Infof("MachineOSBuild %s succeeded, cleaning up all ephemeral objects used for the build", current.Name)
307	
308			mcp, err := b.machineConfigPoolLister.Get(mosc.Spec.MachineConfigPool.Name)
309			if err != nil {
310				return fmt.Errorf("could not get MachineConfigPool from MachineOSConfig %q: %w", mosc.Name, err)
311			}
312	
313			// Update BuildDegraded condition based on current active build status
314			if err := b.updateImageBuildDegradedCondition(ctx, mcp, mosc); err != nil {
315				klog.Errorf("Failed to update ImageBuildDegraded condition for pool %s: %v", mcp.Name, err)
316			}
317	
318			// Clean up ephemeral objects
319			if err := imagebuilder.NewJobImageBuilder(b.kubeclient, b.mcfgclient, current, mosc).Clean(ctx); err != nil {
320				return err
321			}
322	
323			if err := b.updateMachineOSConfigStatus(ctx, mosc, current); err != nil {
324				return fmt.Errorf("could not update MachineOSConfig %q status for successful MachineOSBuild %q: %w", mosc.Name, current.Name, err)
325			}
326		}
327	
328		return nil
329	}

331	// Updates the status on the MachineOSConfig object from the supplied MachineOSBuild object.
332	func (b *buildReconciler) updateMachineOSConfigStatus(ctx context.Context, mosc *mcfgv1.MachineOSConfig, mosb *mcfgv1.MachineOSBuild) error {
333		mosc, err := b.getMachineOSConfigForUpdate(mosc)
334		if err != nil {
335			return err
336		}
337	
338		annoUpdateNeeded := false
339	
340		if hasRebuildAnnotation(mosc) {
341			delete(mosc.Annotations, constants.RebuildMachineOSConfigAnnotationKey)
342			annoUpdateNeeded = true
343			klog.Infof("Cleared rebuild annotation (%q) on MachineOSConfig %q", constants.RebuildMachineOSConfigAnnotationKey, mosc.Name)
344		}
345	
346		if !isCurrentBuildAnnotationEqual(mosc, mosb) {
347			metav1.SetMetaDataAnnotation(&mosc.ObjectMeta, constants.CurrentMachineOSBuildAnnotationKey, mosb.Name)
348			annoUpdateNeeded = true
349			klog.Infof("Set current build on MachineOSConfig %q to MachineOSBuild %q", mosc.Name, mosb.Name)
350		}
351	
352		if annoUpdateNeeded {
353			updatedMosc, err := b.mcfgclient.MachineconfigurationV1().MachineOSConfigs().Update(ctx, mosc, metav1.UpdateOptions{})
354			if err != nil {
355				return fmt.Errorf("could not update annotations on MachineOSConfig %q: %w", mosc.Name, err)
356			}
357	
358			klog.Infof("Updated annotations on MachineOSConfig %q", mosc.Name)
359	
360			mosc = updatedMosc
361	
362			// When annotations are updated (new build starts), also update observedGeneration
363			// to signal that the controller is processing the current generation
364			if mosc.Status.ObservedGeneration != mosc.GetGeneration() {
365				mosc.Status.ObservedGeneration = mosc.GetGeneration()
366				_, err = b.mcfgclient.MachineconfigurationV1().MachineOSConfigs().UpdateStatus(ctx, mosc, metav1.UpdateOptions{})
367				if err != nil {
368					klog.Errorf("Failed to update observedGeneration on MachineOSConfig %q: %v", mosc.Name, err)
369				} else {
370					klog.Infof("Updated observedGeneration on MachineOSConfig %q to %d", mosc.Name, mosc.GetGeneration())
371				}
372			}
373		}
374	
375		// Skip the status update if digest image pushspec hasn't been set yet.
376		if mosb.Status.DigestedImagePushSpec == "" {
377			klog.Infof("MachineOSBuild %q has empty final image pushspec, skipping MachineOSConfig %q status update", mosb.Name, mosc.Name)
378			return nil
379		}
380	
381		// Check if the machineOSBuild reference matches
382		machineOSBuildRefMatches := mosc.Status.MachineOSBuild != nil && mosc.Status.MachineOSBuild.Name == mosb.Name
383	
384		// skip the status update if both the current image pullspec and machineOSBuild reference are already correct.
385		if mosc.Status.CurrentImagePullSpec == mosb.Status.DigestedImagePushSpec && machineOSBuildRefMatches {
386			klog.Infof("MachineOSConfig %q already has final image pushspec and machineOSBuild reference for MachineOSBuild %q", mosc.Name, mosb.Name)
387			return nil
388		}
389	
390		mosc.Status.CurrentImagePullSpec = mosb.Status.DigestedImagePushSpec
391		mosc.Status.ObservedGeneration = mosc.GetGeneration()
392		mosc.Status.MachineOSBuild = &mcfgv1.ObjectReference{
393			Name:     mosb.Name,
394			Group:    mcfgv1.SchemeGroupVersion.Group,
395			Resource: "machineosbuilds",
396		}
397	
398		_, err = b.mcfgclient.MachineconfigurationV1().MachineOSConfigs().UpdateStatus(ctx, mosc, metav1.UpdateOptions{})
399		if err == nil {
400			klog.Infof("Updated status on MachineOSConfig %s", mosc.Name)
401		}
402	
403		return err
404	}

446	// Starts executing a build for a given MachineOSBuild.
447	func (b *buildReconciler) startBuild(ctx context.Context, mosb *mcfgv1.MachineOSBuild) error {
448		mosc, err := utils.GetMachineOSConfigForMachineOSBuild(mosb, b.utilListers())
449		if err != nil {
450			return err
451		}
452	
453		// If there are any other in-progress builds for this MachineOSConfig, stop them first.
454		if err := b.deleteOtherBuildsForMachineOSConfig(ctx, mosb, mosc); err != nil {
455			return fmt.Errorf("could not delete other non-terminal MachineOSBuilds for MachineOSConfig %s: %w", mosc.Name, err)
456		}
457	
458		// Next, create our new MachineOSBuild.
459		if err := imagebuilder.NewJobImageBuilder(b.kubeclient, b.mcfgclient, mosb, mosc).Start(ctx); err != nil {
460			return fmt.Errorf("imagebuilder could not start build for MachineOSBuild %q: %w", mosb.Name, err)
461		}
462	
463		klog.Infof("Started new build %s for MachineOSBuild", utils.GetBuildJobName(mosb))
464	
465		if err := b.updateMachineOSConfigStatus(ctx, mosc, mosb); err != nil {
466			return fmt.Errorf("could not update MachineOSConfig %q status for MachineOSBuild %q: %w", mosc.Name, mosb.Name, err)
467		}
468	
469		// Update BuildDegraded condition based on current active build status when build starts
470		mcp, err := b.machineConfigPoolLister.Get(mosc.Spec.MachineConfigPool.Name)
471		if err != nil {
472			return fmt.Errorf("could not get MachineConfigPool from MachineOSConfig %q: %w", mosc.Name, err)
473		}
474	
475		if err := b.updateImageBuildDegradedCondition(ctx, mcp, mosc); err != nil {
476			klog.Errorf("Failed to update ImageBuildDegraded condition for pool %s: %v", mcp.Name, err)
477		}
478	
479		return nil
480	}

524	// Executes whenever a MachineOSConfig has the rebuild annotation and a new MachineOSBuild needs to be created.
525	func (b *buildReconciler) createNewMachineOSBuildForRebuild(ctx context.Context, mosb *mcfgv1.MachineOSBuild, moscName string) error {
526		// Verify that the MOSB is actually deleted before we try to create a new one
527		// The deletion process may take some time and if we try to create a new MOSB with the same name, a clash may happen
528	
529		// First delete any existing MOSB with exactly this name
530		if err := b.mcfgclient.MachineconfigurationV1().
531			MachineOSBuilds().
532			Delete(ctx, mosb.Name, metav1.DeleteOptions{}); err != nil && !k8serrors.IsNotFound(err) {
533			return fmt.Errorf("could not delete existing MOSB %q: %w", mosb.Name, err)
534		}
535	
536		childCtx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, time.Second*90)
537		defer cancel()
538		for {
539			_, err := b.mcfgclient.MachineconfigurationV1().MachineOSBuilds().Get(childCtx, mosb.Name, metav1.GetOptions{})
540			if err != nil && !k8serrors.IsNotFound(err) {
541				return fmt.Errorf("could not check if MachineOSBuild %s exists: %w", mosb.Name, err)
542			}
543			if k8serrors.IsNotFound(err) {
544				break
545			}
546			time.Sleep(1 * time.Second)
547		}
548	
549		// Delete the digest configmap if it exists
550		// This is created by the wait-for-done container once the image has been built and pushed
551		// and stays around when the build is successful
552		err := b.kubeclient.CoreV1().ConfigMaps(ctrlcommon.MCONamespace).Delete(ctx, utils.GetDigestConfigMapName(mosb), metav1.DeleteOptions{})
553		if err != nil && !k8serrors.IsNotFound(err) {
554			return fmt.Errorf("could not delete digest configmap for MachineOSBuild %s: %w", mosb.Name, err)
555		}
556	
557		// Now create the new MOSB
558		_, err = b.mcfgclient.MachineconfigurationV1().MachineOSBuilds().Create(ctx, mosb, metav1.CreateOptions{})
559		if err != nil {
560			return fmt.Errorf("could not create new MachineOSBuild from rebuild annotation for MachineOSConfig %q: %w", moscName, err)
561		}
562		klog.Infof("New MachineOSBuild created: %s", mosb.Name)
563		return nil
564	}
565	
566	// Executes whenever a new MachineOSBuild is created.
567	func (b *buildReconciler) createNewMachineOSBuildOrReuseExisting(ctx context.Context, mosc *mcfgv1.MachineOSConfig, isRebuild bool) error {
568		mcp, err := b.machineConfigPoolLister.Get(mosc.Spec.MachineConfigPool.Name)
569		if err != nil {
570			return fmt.Errorf("could not get MachineConfigPool %s for MachineOSConfig %s: %w", mosc.Spec.MachineConfigPool.Name, mosc.Name, err)
571		}
572	
573		mc, err := b.machineConfigLister.Get(mcp.Spec.Configuration.Name)
574		if err != nil {
575			return fmt.Errorf("could not get MachineConfig %s for MachineConfigPool %s: %w", mcp.Spec.Configuration.Name, mcp.Name, err)
576		}
577	
578		// Allow builds to retry when pool is degraded only due to BuildDegraded,
579		// but prevent builds for other types of degradation (NodeDegraded, RenderDegraded)
580		if b.shouldPreventBuildDueToDegradation(mcp) {
581			return fmt.Errorf("MachineConfigPool %s is degraded due to non-build issues", mcp.Name)
582		}
583	
584		// Construct a new MachineOSBuild object which has the hashed name attached
585		// to it.
586		mosb, err := buildrequest.NewMachineOSBuild(buildrequest.MachineOSBuildOpts{
587			MachineConfig:     mc,
588			MachineOSConfig:   mosc,
589			MachineConfigPool: mcp,
590		})
591	
592		if err != nil {
593			return fmt.Errorf("could not instantiate new MachineOSBuild: %w", err)
594		}
595	
596		// Set owner reference of the machineOSBuild to the machineOSConfig that created this
597		oref := metav1.NewControllerRef(mosc, mcfgv1.SchemeGroupVersion.WithKind("MachineOSConfig"))
598		mosb.SetOwnerReferences([]metav1.OwnerReference{*oref})
599	
600		existingMosb, err := b.machineOSBuildLister.Get(mosb.Name)
601		if err != nil && !k8serrors.IsNotFound(err) {
602			return fmt.Errorf("could not get MachineOSBuild: %w", err)
603		}
604	
605		if isRebuild {
606			return b.createNewMachineOSBuildForRebuild(ctx, mosb, mosc.Name)
607		}
608	
609		// If err is nil, it means a MachineOSBuild with this name already exists.
610		// What likely happened is that a config change was rolled back to the
611		// previous state. Rather than performing another build, we should get the
612		// previously built image pullspec and adjust the MachineOSConfig to use that
613		// image instead.
614		if err == nil && existingMosb != nil {
615			imageNeedsRebuild, err := b.reuseExistingMachineOSBuildIfPossible(ctx, mosc, existingMosb)
616			if err != nil {
617				return fmt.Errorf("could not reuse existing MachineOSBuild %q for MachineOSConfig %q: %w", existingMosb.Name, mosc.Name, err)
618			}
619	
620			// If we need to rebuild, then we need to create a new MachineOSBuild
621			if imageNeedsRebuild {
622				if err := b.mcfgclient.MachineconfigurationV1().MachineOSBuilds().Delete(ctx, existingMosb.Name, metav1.DeleteOptions{}); err != nil && !k8serrors.IsNotFound(err) {
623					return fmt.Errorf("could not delete existing MOSB %q: %w", existingMosb.Name, err)
624				}
625	
626				return b.createNewMachineOSBuildForRebuild(ctx, mosb, mosc.Name)
627			}
628			// If we did not need to rebuild, then we can reuse the existing MOSB and we are done
629			return nil
630		}
631	
632		// In this situation, we've determined that the MachineOSBuild does not
633		// exist, so we need to create it.
634		if k8serrors.IsNotFound(err) {
635			mosb, err := b.mcfgclient.MachineconfigurationV1().MachineOSBuilds().Create(ctx, mosb, metav1.CreateOptions{})
636			if err != nil && !k8serrors.IsAlreadyExists(err) {
637				return fmt.Errorf("could not create new MachineOSBuild %q: %w", mosb.Name, err)
638			}
639			klog.Infof("New MachineOSBuild created: %s", mosb.Name)
640		}
641	
642		return nil
643	}
644	
645	// Determines if a preexising MachineOSBuild can be reused and if possible, does it.
646	func (b *buildReconciler) reuseExistingMachineOSBuildIfPossible(ctx context.Context, mosc *mcfgv1.MachineOSConfig, existingMosb *mcfgv1.MachineOSBuild) (bool, error) {
647		existingMosbState := ctrlcommon.NewMachineOSBuildState(existingMosb)
648	
649		canBeReused := false
650		imageNeedsRebuild := false
651		// If the existing build is a success and has the image pushspec set, it can be reused.
652		if existingMosbState.IsBuildSuccess() && existingMosb.Status.DigestedImagePushSpec != "" {
653			klog.Infof("Existing MachineOSBuild %q found, checking if image %q still exists", existingMosb.Name, existingMosb.Status.DigestedImagePushSpec)
654	
655			image := string(existingMosb.Spec.RenderedImagePushSpec)
656			inspect, err := b.inspectImage(ctx, image, existingMosb)
657			if inspect != nil && err == nil {
658				klog.Infof("Existing MachineOSBuild %q found, reusing image %q by assigning to MachineOSConfig %q", existingMosb.Name, image, mosc.Name)
659				canBeReused = true
660			} else {
661				klog.Infof("Existing MachineOSBuild image %q no longer exists, skipping reuse. Got error: %v", image, err)
662				imageNeedsRebuild = true
663	
664				// Delete the MOSB so that we can rebuild since the image associated with it doesn't exist anymore
665				klog.Infof("Deleting MachineOSBuild %q so we can rebuild it to create a new image", existingMosb.Name)
666				err := b.mcfgclient.MachineconfigurationV1().MachineOSBuilds().Delete(ctx, existingMosb.Name, metav1.DeleteOptions{})
667				if err != nil && !k8serrors.IsNotFound(err) {
668					return imageNeedsRebuild, fmt.Errorf("could not delete MachineOSBuild %q: %w", existingMosb.Name, err)
669				}
670				return imageNeedsRebuild, nil
671			}
672		}
673	
674		// If the existing build is in a transient state, it can be reused.
675		if existingMosbState.IsInTransientState() {
676			klog.Infof("Existing MachineOSBuild %q found in transient state, assigning to MachineOSConfig %q", existingMosb.Name, mosc.Name)
677			canBeReused = true
678		}
679	
680		if canBeReused {
681			// Stop any other running builds.
682			if err := b.deleteOtherBuildsForMachineOSConfig(ctx, existingMosb, mosc); err != nil {
683				return canBeReused, fmt.Errorf("could not delete running builds for MachineOSConfig %q after reusing existing MachineOSBuild %q: %w", mosc.Name, existingMosb.Name, err)
684			}
685	
686			// Update the MachineOSConfig to use the preexisting MachineOSBuild.
687			if err := b.updateMachineOSConfigStatus(ctx, mosc, existingMosb); err != nil {
688				return canBeReused, fmt.Errorf("could not update MachineOSConfig %q status to reuse preexisting MachineOSBuild %q: %w", mosc.Name, existingMosb.Name, err)
689			}
690		}
691	
692		return imageNeedsRebuild, nil
693	}

695	// Gets the MachineOSBuild status from the provided metav1.Object which can be
696	// converted into a Builder.
697	func (b *buildReconciler) getMachineOSBuildStatusForBuilder(ctx context.Context, obj metav1.Object) (mcfgv1.MachineOSBuildStatus, *mcfgv1.MachineOSBuild, error) {
698		builder, err := buildrequest.NewBuilder(obj)
699		if err != nil {
700			return mcfgv1.MachineOSBuildStatus{}, nil, fmt.Errorf("could not instantiate builder: %w", err)
701		}
702	
703		mosc, mosb, err := b.getMachineOSConfigAndMachineOSBuildForBuilder(builder)
704		if err != nil {
705			return mcfgv1.MachineOSBuildStatus{}, nil, fmt.Errorf("could not get MachineOSConfig or MachineOSBuild for builder: %w", err)
706		}
707	
708		observer := imagebuilder.NewJobImageBuildObserverFromBuilder(b.kubeclient, b.mcfgclient, mosb, mosc, builder)
709	
710		status, err := observer.MachineOSBuildStatus(ctx)
711		if err != nil {
712			return status, mosb, fmt.Errorf("could not get status for MachineOSBuild %q: %w", mosb.Name, err)
713		}
714	
715		return status, mosb, nil
716	}

718	// Gets the status from both the old and current Builder objects before handing
719	// the decision off to setStatusOnMachineOSBuildIfNeeded.
720	func (b *buildReconciler) updateMachineOSBuildWithStatusIfNeeded(ctx context.Context, oldBuilder, curBuilder metav1.Object) error {
721		oldStatus, _, err := b.getMachineOSBuildStatusForBuilder(ctx, oldBuilder)
722		if err != nil {
723			// If we can't find the MachineOSConfig, MachineOSBuild, or any of the
724			// ephemeral build objects, it means that it was probably deleted. Instead
725			// of trying to reconcile the status, we'll return nil here to avoid
726			// requeueing another attempt.
727			return ignoreErrIsNotFound(fmt.Errorf("could not get status for old builder: %w", err))
728		}
729	
730		curStatus, mosb, err := b.getMachineOSBuildStatusForBuilder(ctx, curBuilder)
731		if err != nil {
732			// If we can't find the MachineOSConfig, MachineOSBuild, or any of the
733			// ephemeral build objects, it means that it was probably deleted. Instead
734			// of trying to reconcile the status, we'll return nil here to avoid
735			// requeueing another attempt.
736			return ignoreErrIsNotFound(fmt.Errorf("could not get status for current builder: %w", err))
737		}
738	
739		mosbCreation := mosb.GetCreationTimestamp()
740		builderCreation := curBuilder.GetCreationTimestamp()
741	
742		// It is possible that the build pod can be newer than the MachineOSBuild.
743		// This is the case whenever the MachineOSBuild is deleted, the underlying
744		// build objects (pod, ephemeral build objects, etc.) get deleted and then
745		// recreated while the MachineOBuild gets created as well.
746		//
747		// When this happens, the MachineOSBuild can go into the "interrupted" state
748		// and would require intervention to delete and retry enough times for a new
749		// build to start.
750		//
751		// A better solution for this would be to update the BuilderReference status
752		// field on the MachineOSBuild to include the ID of the build pod so that we
753		// can reconcile that more effectively. Alternatively, using a generated name
754		// for the builder pod would also ensure that we don't have to wait for one
755		// to be deleted before another can be created.
756		if builderCreation.Before(&mosbCreation) && curBuilder.GetDeletionTimestamp() != nil {
757			klog.Infof("Builder %q has deletion timestamp and is newer than MachineOSBuild %q, skipping update", curBuilder.GetName(), mosb.GetName())
758			return nil
759		}
760	
761		if err := b.setStatusOnMachineOSBuildIfNeeded(ctx, mosb, oldStatus, curStatus); err != nil {
762			return fmt.Errorf("could not set status on MachineOSBuild %q: %w", mosb.Name, err)
763		}
764	
765		return nil
766	}
767	
768	// Sets the status on the MachineOSBuild object after comparing the statuses according to very specific state transitions.
769	func (b *buildReconciler) setStatusOnMachineOSBuildIfNeeded(ctx context.Context, mosb *mcfgv1.MachineOSBuild, oldStatus, curStatus mcfgv1.MachineOSBuildStatus) error {
770		// Compare the old status and the current status to determine if an update is
771		// needed. This is handled according to very specific state transitions.
772		isUpdateNeeded, reason := isMachineOSBuildStatusUpdateNeeded(oldStatus, curStatus)
773		if !isUpdateNeeded {
774			if reason != "" {
775				klog.Infof("MachineOSBuild %q %s; skipping update because of invalid transition", mosb.Name, reason)
776			}
777	
778			return nil
779		}
780	
781		klog.Infof("MachineOSBuild %q %s; update needed", mosb.Name, reason)
782	
783		mosb, err := b.getMachineOSBuildForUpdate(mosb)
784		if err != nil {
785			return err
786		}
787	
788		bs := ctrlcommon.NewMachineOSBuildState(mosb)
789	
790		bs.SetBuildConditions(curStatus.Conditions)
791	
792		bs.Build.Status.DigestedImagePushSpec = curStatus.DigestedImagePushSpec
793	
794		if bs.Build.Status.BuildStart == nil && curStatus.BuildStart != nil {
795			bs.Build.Status.BuildStart = curStatus.BuildStart
796		}
797	
798		if bs.Build.Status.BuildEnd == nil && curStatus.BuildEnd != nil {
799			bs.Build.Status.BuildEnd = curStatus.BuildEnd
800		}
801	
802		bs.Build.Status.Builder = curStatus.Builder
803	
804		_, err = b.mcfgclient.MachineconfigurationV1().MachineOSBuilds().UpdateStatus(ctx, bs.Build, metav1.UpdateOptions{})
805		if err != nil {
806			return fmt.Errorf("could not update status on MachineOSBuild %q: %w", mosb.Name, err)
807		}
808	
809		klog.Infof("Updated status on MachineOSBuild %s", bs.Build.Name)
810		return nil
811	}
812	
813	// Gets the status from the running builder and applies it to the MachineOSBuild.
814	func (b *buildReconciler) updateMachineOSBuildWithStatus(ctx context.Context, obj metav1.Object) error {
815		curStatus, mosb, err := b.getMachineOSBuildStatusForBuilder(ctx, obj)
816		if err != nil {
817			// If we can't find the MachineOSConfig, MachineOSBuild, or any of the
818			// ephemeral build objects, it means that it was probably deleted. Instead
819			// of trying to reconcile the status, we'll return nil here to avoid
820			// requeueing another attempt.
821			return ignoreErrIsNotFound(fmt.Errorf("could not update MachineOSBuild with status: %w", err))
822		}
823	
824		// Compare the status returned from the builder to the status on the
825		// MachineOSBuild object from the lister to determine if an update is needed
826		// since we don't have an older build status to compare it to.
827		if err := b.setStatusOnMachineOSBuildIfNeeded(ctx, mosb, mosb.Status, curStatus); err != nil {
828			return fmt.Errorf("unable to set status on MachineOSBuild %q: %w", mosb.Name, err)
829		}
830	
831		return nil
832	}

879	// Deletes the underlying build objects for a given MachineOSBuild.
880	func (b *buildReconciler) deleteBuilderForMachineOSBuild(ctx context.Context, mosb *mcfgv1.MachineOSBuild) error {
881		if err := imagebuilder.NewJobImageBuildCleaner(b.kubeclient, b.mcfgclient, mosb).Clean(ctx); err != nil {
882			return fmt.Errorf("could not clean build %s: %w", mosb.Name, err)
883		}
884		// Delete the image associated with the MOSB first
885		moscName, err := utils.GetRequiredLabelValueFromObject(mosb, constants.MachineOSConfigNameLabelKey)
886		if err != nil {
887			klog.Warningf("could not get MachineOSConfig name for MachineOSBuild %s: %v, cannot delete image", mosb.Name, err)
888			return nil
889		}
890		if err := b.deleteMOSBImage(ctx, mosb, moscName); err != nil {
891			return err
892		}
893		// Delete the digest configmap if it exists
894		// This is created by the wait-for-done container once the image has been built and pushed
895		// and stays around when the build is successful
896		err = b.kubeclient.CoreV1().ConfigMaps(ctrlcommon.MCONamespace).Delete(ctx, utils.GetDigestConfigMapName(mosb), metav1.DeleteOptions{})
897		if err != nil && !k8serrors.IsNotFound(err) {
898			return fmt.Errorf("could not delete digest configmap for MachineOSBuild %s for MachineOSConfig %s: %w", mosb.Name, moscName, err)
899		}
900		return nil
901	}

904	func (b *buildReconciler) deleteMachineOSBuild(ctx context.Context, mosb *mcfgv1.MachineOSBuild) error {
905		moscName, err := utils.GetRequiredLabelValueFromObject(mosb, constants.MachineOSConfigNameLabelKey)
906		if err != nil {
907			moscName = "<unknown MachineOSConfig>"
908		}
909		// Delete the image associated with the MOSB first
910		if err := b.deleteMOSBImage(ctx, mosb, moscName); err != nil {
911			return err
912		}
913	
914		// Delete the digest configmap if it exists
915		// This is created by the wait-for-done container once the image has been built and pushed
916		// and stays around when the build is successful
917		err = b.kubeclient.CoreV1().ConfigMaps(ctrlcommon.MCONamespace).Delete(ctx, utils.GetDigestConfigMapName(mosb), metav1.DeleteOptions{})
918		if err != nil && !k8serrors.IsNotFound(err) {
919			return fmt.Errorf("could not delete digest configmap for MachineOSBuild %s for MachineOSConfig %s: %w", mosb.Name, moscName, err)
920		}
921	
922		err = b.mcfgclient.MachineconfigurationV1().MachineOSBuilds().Delete(ctx, mosb.Name, metav1.DeleteOptions{})
923		if err == nil {
924			klog.Infof("Deleted MachineOSBuild %s for MachineOSConfig %s", mosb.Name, moscName)
925			return nil
926		}
927	
928		if k8serrors.IsNotFound(err) {
929			klog.Infof("MachineOSBuild %s was not found for MachineOSConfig %s", mosb.Name, moscName)
930			return nil
931		}

933		return fmt.Errorf("could not delete MachineOSBuild %s for MachineOSConfig %s: %w", mosb.Name, moscName, err)

936	func (b *buildReconciler) deleteMOSBImage(ctx context.Context, mosb *mcfgv1.MachineOSBuild, moscName string) error {
937		moscExists := true
938		_, err := b.listers.machineOSConfigLister.Get(moscName)
939		if k8serrors.IsNotFound(err) {
940			moscExists = false
941		} else if err != nil {
942			return fmt.Errorf("could not get MachineOSConfig for MachineOSBuild %q: %w", mosb.Name, err)
943		}
944	
945		if moscExists {
946			pool, err := b.listers.machineConfigPoolLister.Get(mosb.ObjectMeta.Labels[constants.TargetMachineConfigPoolLabelKey])
947			if err != nil {
948				return fmt.Errorf("could not get MachineConfigPool from MachineOSBuild %q: %w", mosb.Name, err)
949			}
950	
951			nodes, err := helpers.GetNodesForPool(b.listers.machineConfigPoolLister, b.listers.nodeLister, pool)
952			if err != nil {
953				return fmt.Errorf("could not get nodes for MachineConfigPool %q: %w", pool.Name, err)
954			}
955	
956			for _, node := range nodes {
957				if node.GetAnnotations()[daemonconstants.CurrentImageAnnotationKey] == string(mosb.Status.DigestedImagePushSpec) ||
958					node.GetAnnotations()[daemonconstants.DesiredImageAnnotationKey] == string(mosb.Status.DigestedImagePushSpec) {
959					// the image we are trying to delete is currently on a node or desired by a node
960					klog.Warningf("Image %s is currently applied on a node or desired by a node, will not delete", string(mosb.Status.DigestedImagePushSpec))
961					return nil
962				}
963			}
964		}
965	
966		image := string(mosb.Spec.RenderedImagePushSpec)
967		if err := b.deleteImage(ctx, image, mosb); err != nil {
968			wrappedErr := fmt.Errorf("could not delete image for MachineOSBuild %s for MachineOSConfig %s: %w", mosb.Name, moscName, err)
969			// If the image cannot be deleted because it either does not exist or one's
970			// creds do not have the necessary permissions, then we should ignore the
971			// error and continue.
972			if imagepruner.IsTolerableDeleteErr(err) || k8serrors.IsNotFound(err) {
973				klog.Warning(wrappedErr.Error())
974			} else {
975				return wrappedErr
976			}
977		} else {
978			klog.Infof("Deleted image %s from registry for MachineOSBuild %s", image, mosb.Name)
979		}
980	
981		return nil
982	}

985	func (b *buildReconciler) deleteOtherBuildsForMachineOSConfig(ctx context.Context, newMosb *mcfgv1.MachineOSBuild, mosc *mcfgv1.MachineOSConfig) error {

989		}
990	
991		for _, mosb := range mosbList {
992			// Ignore the newly-created MachineOSBuild.
993			if mosb.Name == newMosb.Name {
994				continue
995			}
996	
997			mosbState := ctrlcommon.NewMachineOSBuildState(mosb)
998	
999			// If the build is in any other state except for "success", delete it.
1000			if !mosbState.IsBuildSuccess() {
1001				klog.Infof("Found running MachineOSBuild %s for MachineOSConfig %s, deleting...", mosb.Name, mosc.Name)
1002				if err := b.deleteMachineOSBuild(ctx, mosb); err != nil {
1003					return fmt.Errorf("could not delete running MachineOSBuild %s: %w", mosb.Name, err)
1004				}
1005			}
1006		}

1008		return nil
1009	}

1	# Machine OS Builder Design
2	
3	In the context of the Machine Config Operator (MCO) in Red Hat
4	OpenShift, on-cluster builds refer to the process of building an OS
5	image directly on the OpenShift cluster, rather than building them
6	outside the cluster (such as on a local machine or continuous
7	integration (CI) pipeline) and then making a configuration change so
8	that the cluster uses them. By doing this, we enable cluster
9	administrators to have more control over the contents and configuration
10	of their clusters’ OS image through a familiar interface (MachineConfigs
11	and in the future, Dockerfiles).
12	
13	![MOB Diagram](./MachineOSBuilderDiagram.svg)
14	
15	## Sub-components and design
16	
17	On-cluster builds are achieved with the help of several subcomponents
18	that work in concert with one another. This is a brief overview of what
19	they do and how they work:
20	
21	### Machine OS Builder (MOB)
22	
23	This is an additional binary which forms a part of the Machine Config
24	Operator. By implementing as much of the on-cluster build functionality
25	within the MOB binary, we can ensure that the code paths are only
26	reached whenever a cluster admin explicitly opts into OS layering. This
27	top-level component (and its subcomponents described below) is only
28	started whenever a cluster administrator opts in. If a cluster
29	administrator opts out, it will gracefully shut down. The below
30	subcomponents (BuildController, ImageBuildController,
31	PodBuildController) are part of the MOB binary.
32	
33	### BuildController
34	
35	BuildController is the entrypoint for determining whenever an OS build
36	is required as well as ingesting the user-provided configuration details
37	(described below). BuildController listens for changes to
38	MachineConfigPools and determines whether a change requires a new OS
39	image build. At the time of this writing, only a rendered MachineConfig
40	change will cause a new build to be performed. Other changes to
41	MachineConfigPools will be ignored at this time. It is worth noting that
42	BuildController only determines whether a build is needed, makes the
43	necessary preparations, starts the build, and updates the
44	MachineConfigPool with the current build state. Upon completion, it will
45	retrieve the image pullspec from the build process and update the
46	MachineConfigPool object with the latest image pullspec.
47	
48	To actually perform the build, BuildController makes use of either the
49	ImageBuildController or the PodBuildController as described below. It is
50	important to understand that ImageBuildController and PodBuildController
51	are designed to be as interchangeable as possible. Additional image
52	builders may be produced in the future to support additional use-cases
53	and to optimize the build process within certain OpenShift contexts
54	(e.g., single-node). However for now, we have the two described below.
55	
56	### ImageBuildController
57	
58	ImageBuildController makes use of the OpenShift Image Builder
59	functionality which is currently a core feature of OpenShift. When this
60	feature is present on ones’ OpenShift cluster, BuildController will
61	start an ImageBuildController which creates an OpenShift Build object to
62	perform the build. Because OpenShift Image Builder will eventually
63	become an optional component, it makes sense that we should have a
64	second mechanism for clusters which do not have the OpenShift Image
65	Builder capability enabled. Using OpenShift Image Builder allows us to
66	avoid implementing this specific functionality itself and has the
67	additional benefit that the OpenShift Image Builder components are
68	lifecycled and versioned along with the rest of the OpenShift release
69	process.
70	
71	### PodBuildController
72	
73	PodBuildController enables builds by creating a custom build pod, which
74	uses the officially published Buildah image
75	(`quay.io/buildah/stable:latest`). Additionally, PodBuildController
76	attempts to run with the fewest privileges possible. To do so, the
77	`machine-os-builder` service account (which MOB runs as) sets the `anyuid`
78	Security Context Constraint. This allows us to map the build UID
79	contained within the Buildah container to a UID on our cluster,
80	effectively allowing us to run as an unprivileged pod, without root
81	permissions. A limitation of using Buildah is that to get the final
82	image digest after a push operation, we must write a file to the
83	filesystem (called a `digestfile` in Buildah parlance). To get this value
84	back to BuildController, we add a second container (called
85	`wait-for-done`) onto our build pod which waits for the `digestfile` to be
86	written to the emptyDir volume shared between the wait-for-done
87	container and the image-build container. Once the `digestfile` exists,
88	the `wait-for-done` container uses the `oc` binary built into the base OS
89	image to create a ConfigMap containing the `digestfile`. The
90	PodBuildController will then read this ConfigMap, get the digestfile
91	contents, and delete the ConfigMap once the build is successful. It will
92	then construct a fully-qualified image pullspec based upon the
93	user-provided final OS image pullspec and the digest retrieved from the
94	digestfile.
95	
96	## Detailed flow
97	
98	Once a cluster administrator opts a MachineConfigPool in to OS layering,
99	the following describes the typical build process:
100	
101	1. The MCO will set up all of the service accounts, RBAC rules, etc. to
102	   allow the MOB to perform its functions. It will then start a
103	   single replica of the MOB process.
104	
105	2. For now, the MOB process will start a BuildController which uses the
106	   ImageBuildController. This will change in the future (see
107	   [Known Limitations](OnClusterBuildInstructions.md#known-limitations)
108	   for details).
109	
110	3. BuildController looks for MachineConfigPools that have been opted
111	   into OS layering, looking for the existence of the
112	   `machineconfiguration.openshift.io/layering-enabled` label on all
113	   MachineConfigPools. It will create one build process for each
114	   MachineConfigPool opted in, using the rendered MachineConfig name
115	   as a unique identifier for all of the objects it creates. Each
116	   build process lives in its own build pod with its own unique
117	   inputs.
118	
119	4. BuildController will read the `on-cluster-build-config` ConfigMap to
120	   get the base OS image pull secret, the final OS image push secret,
121	   and the pullspec for the final OS image. If necessary, the push
122	   and pull secrets will be converted from `.dockercfg` to
123	   `.dockerjsoncfg`.
124	
125	5. BuildController will read the `machine-config-osimageurl` ConfigMap to
126	   get the base OS image and extensions image pullspecs. These will
127	   be used to render the Dockerfile. If no extensions image is
128	   present, the section of the Dockerfile which requires the
129	   extensions image will not be rendered.
130	
131	6. The Dockerfile will be rendered and temporarily stored as a
132	   ConfigMap within the MCO namespace for the lifetime of the OS
133	   image build process.
134	
135	7. The rendered MachineConfig will be gzipped and base64-encoded and
136	   temporarily stored as a ConfigMap within the MCO namespace for the
137	   lifetime of the OS image build process. Because ConfigMaps can
138	   only store a maximum of 1 MB, the MachineConfig is gzipped and
139	   Base64-encoded before storing in a ConfigMap. The Base64-encoding
140	   is required because ConfigMaps are not intended to store binary
141	   data, which is what one gets after gzipping. (see:
142	   [Known Limitations](OnClusterBuildInstructions.md#known-limitations)).
143	
144	8. Either a new OpenShift Image Build or custom build pod will be
145	   created to consume the Dockerfile and the rendered MachineConfig
146	   ConfigMaps to perform the build. The rendered Dockerfile will be
147	   exactly the same regardless of which builder is used (although in
148	   the future, we could add a label indicating which image builder
149	   was used). The Dockerfile essentially copies the rendered
150	   MachineConfig from the ConfigMap into the base image,
151	   Base64-decodes and gunzips it, then calls ignition live-apply to
152	   write the MachineConfig contents to the OS image. Although there
153	   is a section to perform operations on the extensions image, it
154	   does not currently do anything other than pull the extensions
155	   image (see:
156	   [Known Limitations](OnClusterBuildInstructions.md#known-limitations)).
157	
158	9. Upon successful completion of the build, the image is pushed to the
159	   configured image registry, using the rendered MachineConfig name
160	   as a tag. For example:
161	   `quay.io/myorg/myrepo:rendered-worker-dd37180cefa6a1e88834966330c0c028`).
162	   However, the SHA256 digest which points to this tag is obtained
163	   and used to create a fully-qualified pullspec. Any user-supplied
164	   tags will be ignored. Obtaining the final image pullspec is done
165	   in one of two ways:
166	
167	   - For `ImageBuildController` builds, this value is retrieved from
168	     the OpenShift Image Build object and returned.
169	
170	   - For `PodBuildController` builds, a second container process waits
171	     for the Buildah digestfile to be written to an emptyDir
172	     volume. Once written, the second container process will write
173	     the digestfile contents to an ephemeral ConfigMap in the MCO
174	     namespace which is later retrieved and deleted by the
175	     PodBuildController.
176	
177	10. Once the final image pullspec is obtained, an annotation
178	    (`machineconfiguration.openshift.io/newestImageEquivalentConfig`) on
179	    the MachineConfigPool object is updated with the fully-qualified
180	    pullspec (e.g.,
181	    `quay.io/myorg/myrepo@sha256:324f17d18b197a951e7e11ea0b101836312191f92aefa0fc2ee240354cfbf7fc`).
182	    Additionally, the `BuildSuccess` status condition is set on the
183	    MachineConfigPool.
184	
185	11. The BuildController then deletes all of the ephemeral objects it
186	    created to perform the build. This includes the build pod object
187	    or OpenShift Image Build object, the rendered Dockerfile
188	    ConfigMap, the rendered MachineConfig ConfigMap, as well as the
189	    `digestfile` ConfigMap (if created). In cases where a build was
190	    unsuccessful, these objects will not be deleted to allow a cluster
191	    administrator the opportunity to review / gather the logs as well
192	    as the inputs into the process.
193	
194	12. The MachineConfigPool annotation will be rolled out
195	    to all of the target nodes by the Node Controller using a similar
196	    process to how MachineConfigs are currently rolled out.
197	
198	# Frequently Asked Questions
199	
200	## What labels are added onto the final OS image?
201	
202	Image labels indicating what MachineConfigPool this targets, the rendered
203	MachineConfig used to produce the configs, the base OS image pullspec, and
204	extensions OS image pullspec (when available) are added onto the final OS image.
205	This will enable future optimizations and enhancements by enabling a build to be
206	skipped whenever we already have an appropriate image built for that
207	MachineConfig.
208

1	# On-Cluster Layering Quickstart Guide
2	
3	## Prerequisites
4	This quick-start guide assumes you have the following:
5	- Access to an OpenShift 4.16+ cluster with TechPreview mode enabled.
6	- We will use an ImageStream for final image storage and retrieval. You can use an external image registry such as Quay.io, if desired.
7	- The OpenShift CLI tool (`oc`)
8	- [`yq`](https://github.com/mikefarah/yq)
9	
10	## Background
11	
12	### MachineOSConfig
13	
14	The MachineOSConfig is the entrypoint into On-Cluster Layering (OCL). This is where you can specify what Containerfile to build, which MachineConfigPool to associate the build with, where the final image should be pushed and pulled from, as well as the secrets to use for those purposes. It's schema looks like this:
15	
16	```yaml
17	---
18	apiVersion: machineconfiguration.openshift.io/v1alpha1
19	kind: MachineOSConfig
20	metadata:
21	  name: layered
22	spec:
23	  # Here is where you refer to the MachineConfigPool that you want your built
24	  # image to be deployed to.
25	  machineConfigPool:
26	    name: layered
27	  buildInputs:
28	    containerFile:
29	    # Here is where you can set the Containerfile for your MachineConfigPool.
30	    # You'll note that you need to provide an architecture. This is because this
31	    # will eventually support multiarch clusters. For now, only noArch is
32	    # supported.
33	    - containerfileArch: noarch
34	      content: |-
35	        <containerfile contents>
36	    # Here is where you can select an image builder type. For now, we only
37	    # support the "PodImageBuilder" type that we maintain ourselves. Future
38	    # integrations can / will include other build system integrations.
39	    imageBuilder:
40	      imageBuilderType: PodImageBuilder
41	    # The Machine OS Builder needs to know what pull secret it can use to pull
42	    # the base OS image.
43	    baseImagePullSecret:
44	      name: <secret-name>
45	    # Here is where you specify the name of the push secret you use to push
46	    # your newly-built image to.
47	    renderedImagePushSecret:
48	      name: <secret-name>
49	    # Here is where you specify the image registry to push your newly-built
50	    # images to.
51	    renderedImagePushspec: <final image pullspec>
52	  buildOutputs:
53	    # Here is where you specify what image will be used on all of your nodes to
54	    # pull the newly-built image.
55	    currentImagePullSecret:
56	      name: <secret-name>
57	```
58	
59	There is a 1:1 relationship between a MachineOSConfig and a MachineConfigPool. It is possible to opt-in only a single MachineConfigPool.
60	
61	### MachineOSBuild
62	
63	A `MachineOSBuild` instance represents a specific build associated with a MachineOSConfig. As a cluster admin, you will not need to worry about directly interacting with these for now. However, their presence can be used to determine what state a given build is in, such as whether it was successful, where to pull the image from, and what MachineConfig was built into the image.
64	
65	## Getting Started
66	
67	For the sake of this walk-through, we will create a MachineConfigPool called `layered` and we will associate a MachineOSConfig (also named `layered`) with this MachineConfigPool. Both the MachineConfigPool and the MachineOSConfig can be named anything one desires; however for the sake of this walk-through, we will use the name `layered`. We will also be using an ImageStream as our image registry although you are free to use an external image registry, if desired.
68	
69	### Initial Setup
70	
71	First, we'll create the necessary objects that we'll need in advance:
72	
73	```bash
74	#!/usr/bin/env bash
75	
76	# (required): Clone the global image pull secret into a secret called
77	# "global-pull-secret-copy" within the MCO namespace. This will be used to pull
78	# the base OS image:
79	oc create secret docker-registry global-pull-secret-copy \
80	  --namespace "openshift-machine-config-operator" \
81	  --from-file=.dockerconfigjson=<(oc get secret/pull-secret -n openshift-config -o go-template='{{index .data ".dockerconfigjson" | base64decode}}')
82	
83	# (required for OCP): Clone the Red Hat entitlements certificate into the MCO
84	# namespace. This will be used to access RHEL content that you are entitled to
85	# access.
86	oc create secret generic etc-pki-entitlement \
87	  --namespace "openshift-machine-config-operator" \
88	  --from-file=entitlement.pem=<(oc get secret/etc-pki-entitlement -n openshift-config-managed -o go-template='{{index .data "entitlement.pem" | base64decode }}') \
89	  --from-file=entitlement-key.pem=<(oc get secret/etc-pki-entitlement -n openshift-config-managed -o go-template='{{index .data "entitlement-key.pem" | base64decode }}')
90	
91	# (optional): Create an ImageStream which will act as our image registry. This
92	# step can be omitted if you are using an external image registry such as
93	# Quay.io, though you will need to create the push and pull secrets in Quay.io
94	# as well as inside your cluster.
95	oc create imagestream os-images -n openshift-machine-config-operator
96	
97	# (optional): To get the image registry pullspec for your ImageStream, you can run the following command:
98	# This will return "image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-machine-config-operator/os-images"
99	oc get imagestream/os-images  -n openshift-machine-config-operator -o=jsonpath='{.status.dockerImageRepository}'
100	
101	# (optional): Since this walk-through will use an ImageStream, we need to get the push and
102	# pull secret associated with the builder service account. For the sake of this
103	# demonstration, lets assume that this command returns the name
104	# "builder-dockercfg-123".
105	#
106	# If using an external image registry, this step can be omitted, although you
107	# will still need to create the push and pull secrets in the MCO namespace.
108	oc get secrets -o name -n openshift-machine-config-operator -o=jsonpath='{.items[?(@.metadata.annotations.openshift\.io\/internal-registry-auth-token\.service-account=="builder")].metadata.name}'
109	
110	# (required): Create the layered MachineConfigPool:
111	cat << EOF | oc create -f -
112	---
113	apiVersion: machineconfiguration.openshift.io/v1
114	kind: MachineConfigPool
115	metadata:
116	  name: layered
117	spec:
118	  machineConfigSelector:
119	    matchExpressions:
120	      - key: machineconfiguration.openshift.io/role
121	        operator: In
122	        values:
123	        - worker
124	        - layered
125	  nodeSelector:
126	    matchLabels:
127	      node-role.kubernetes.io/layered: ""
128	EOF
129	```
130	
131	### Customized OS Image
132	
133	We will use the following Containerfile to install some useful packages on our cluster nodes:
134	
135	```Dockerfile
136	FROM configs AS final
137	RUN rpm-ostree install tree && \
138	    ostree container commit
139	```
140	
141	A couple of notes about this Containerfile:
142	
143	- Multiple build stages are allowed.
144	
145	- One must use the configs image target (`FROM configs AS final`) to
146	  inject content into it and it must be the last image in the build.
147	
148	- It is injected after the MachineConfigs are layered into the base OS
149	  image.
150	
151	- If pulling additional images from a private container registry is
152	  required, those pull credentials must be included within the secret referred
153	  to in the `baseImagePullSecret` config key.
154	
155	### Create the MachineOSConfig
156	
157	Finally, we create the MachineOSConfig object. For the sake of this walk-through, we will assume that the push / pull secret is named `builder-dockercfg-123` and we will assume that the name of the base image pull secret is `global-pull-secret-copy`. Although we will use [`yq`](https://github.com/mikefarah/yq) to create this file, you are welcome to use your favorite text editor instead:
158	
159	```bash
160	#!/usr/bin/env bash
161	
162	# Write the sample MachineOSConfig to a YAML file:
163	cat << EOF > layered-machineosconfig.yaml
164	---
165	apiVersion: machineconfiguration.openshift.io/v1alpha1
166	kind: MachineOSConfig
167	metadata:
168	  name: layered
169	spec:
170	  # Here is where you refer to the MachineConfigPool that you want your built
171	  # image to be deployed to.
172	  machineConfigPool:
173	    name: layered
174	  buildInputs:
175	    containerFile:
176	    # Here is where you can set the Containerfile for your MachineConfigPool.
177	    # You'll note that you need to provide an architecture. This is because this
178	    # will eventually support multiarch clusters. For now, only noArch is
179	    # supported.
180	    - containerfileArch: noarch
181	      content: |-
182	        <containerfile contents>
183	    # Here is where you can select an image builder type. For now, we only
184	    # support the "PodImageBuilder" type that we maintain ourselves. Future
185	    # integrations can / will include other build system integrations.
186	    imageBuilder:
187	      imageBuilderType: PodImageBuilder
188	    # The Machine OS Builder needs to know what pull secret it can use to pull
189	    # the base OS image.
190	    baseImagePullSecret:
191	      name: <secret-name>
192	    # Here is where you specify the name of the push secret you use to push
193	    # your newly-built image to.
194	    renderedImagePushSecret:
195	      name: <secret-name>
196	    # Here is where you specify the image registry to push your newly-built
197	    # images to.
198	    renderedImagePushspec: <final image pullspec>
199	  buildOutputs:
200	    # Here is where you specify what image will be used on all of your nodes to
201	    # pull the newly-built image.
202	    currentImagePullSecret:
203	      name: <secret-name>
204	EOF
205	
206	# Write the Containerfile to a file:
207	cat << EOF > Containerfile
208	FROM configs AS final
209	RUN rpm-ostree install tree && \
210	    ostree container commit
211	EOF
212	
213	# Finally, we'll modify our file using yq (https://github.com/mikefarah/yq).
214	
215	# This is the name of the secret that will be used to push the built image to
216	# the image registry.
217	export pushSecretName="builder-dockercfg-123"
218	
219	# This is the name of the secret that will be used to pull the built image from
220	# the image registry onto each node.
221	export pullSecretName="builder-dockercfg-123"
222	
223	# This is the name of the secret that will be used to pull the base OS image to
224	# the build pod to be consumed during the build.
225	export baseImagePullSecretName="global-pull-secret-copy"
226	
227	# This has the contents of the Containerfile.
228	export containerfileContents="$(cat Containerfile)"
229	
230	# Notice that we added ":latest" onto our image registry pullspec. This tag will
231	# not actually be used.
232	export imageRegistryPullspec="image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-machine-config-operator/os-images:latest"
233	
234	
235	yq -i e '.spec.buildInputs.baseImagePullSecret.name = strenv(baseImagePullSecretName)' ./layered-machineosconfig.yaml
236	yq -i e '.spec.buildInputs.renderedImagePushSecret.name = strenv(pushSecretName)' ./layered-machineosconfig.yaml
237	yq -i e '.spec.buildOutputs.currentImagePullSecret.name = strenv(pullSecretName)' ./layered-machineosconfig.yaml
238	yq -i e '.spec.buildInputs.containerFile[0].content = strenv(containerfileContents)' ./layered-machineosconfig.yaml
239	yq -i e '.spec.buildInputs.renderedImagePushspec = strenv(imageRegistryPullspec)' ./layered-machineosconfig.yaml
240	```
241	
242	This yields the following YAML:
243	
244	```yaml
245	---
246	apiVersion: machineconfiguration.openshift.io/v1alpha1
247	kind: MachineOSConfig
248	metadata:
249	  name: layered
250	spec:
251	  # Here is where you refer to the MachineConfigPool that you want your built
252	  # image to be deployed to.
253	  machineConfigPool:
254	    name: layered
255	  buildInputs:
256	    containerFile:
257	      # Here is where you can set the Containerfile for your MachineConfigPool.
258	      # You'll note that you need to provide an architecture. This is because
259	      # this will eventually support multiarch clusters.
260	      - containerfileArch: noarch
261	        content: |-
262	          FROM configs AS final
263	          RUN rpm-ostree install tree && \
264	            ostree container commit
265	    # Here is where you can select an image builder type. For now, we only
266	    # support a pod image builder that we maintain ourselves. Future
267	    # integrations can / will include other build system integrations.
268	    imageBuilder:
269	      imageBuilderType: PodImageBuilder
270	    # The Machine OS Builder needs to know what pull secret it can use to pull
271	    # the base OS image.
272	    baseImagePullSecret:
273	      name: global-pull-secret-copy
274	    # Here is where you specify the name of the push secret you use to push
275	    # your newly-built image to.
276	    renderedImagePushSecret:
277	      name: builder-dockercfg-123
278	    # Here is where you specify the image registry to push your newly-built
279	    # images to. In this example, we're using an ImageStream, but one can
280	    # easily use an image registry such as Quay.io.
281	    renderedImagePushspec: image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-machine-config-operator/os-image:latest
282	  buildOutputs:
283	    # Here is where you specify what image will be used on all of your nodes to
284	    # pull the newly-built image.
285	    currentImagePullSecret:
286	      name: builder-dockercfg-123
287	```

289	Now that we've done this, we can apply the layered MachineOSConfig to our cluster:

482	
483	## Conclusion
484	
485	At this point, we now have a customized OS image installed on our cluster nodes. If the MachineConfigs for the `layered` MachineConfigPool are changed or the `Containerfile` is changed, a new `MachineOSBuild` will be created, the build will automatically start, and the image will be rolled out automatically to all of the nodes within the `layered` MachineConfigPool.
486	
487	While this is a contrived example of installing a small helper tool, there are more useful use-cases such as installing device drivers, monitoring tools, etc.

1	# ADR-001: On-Cluster Layering (OCL)
2	
3	## Status
4	Implemented (TechPreview)
5	
6	## Context
7	Traditional MCO updates apply Ignition configs and use rpm-ostree to manage the OS. Users need to add custom packages or content to RHCOS nodes but have no supported mechanism to build custom OS images integrated with the MCO update lifecycle.
8	
9	## Decision
10	Introduce On-Cluster Layering: users create a `MachineOSConfig` with a Containerfile, MCO builds a custom OS image on-cluster via `MachineOSBuild` Jobs (podman build), and the resulting image is applied to nodes in the target pool.
11	
12	## Components
13	- `MachineOSConfig` CRD: build inputs (Containerfile, pool ref)
14	- `MachineOSBuild` CRD: build execution tracking
15	- `machine-os-builder` binary: orchestrates builds
16	- Build Controller (`pkg/controller/build/`): reconciles MachineOSBuild lifecycle
17	- Image Builder (`pkg/controller/build/imagebuilder/`): podman/buildah job creation
18	- Image Pruner (`pkg/controller/build/imagepruner/`): cleanup of old images
19	
20	## Consequences
21	- Node Controller must coordinate with build status before rolling updates
22	- MachineConfigPool gains build-related conditions (BuildPending, Building, BuildSuccess, BuildFailed)
23	- Daemon must support bootc-based updates in addition to rpm-ostree
24	- Feature-gated CRD variants needed for gradual rollout
25

1	# MCO Architecture - Component Internals
2	
3	## Binaries
4	
5	MCO produces 7 binaries from `cmd/`:
6	
7	| Binary | Deployment | Purpose |
8	|--------|-----------|---------|
9	| `machine-config-operator` | Deployment | Top-level operator: syncs all sub-components, manages ClusterOperator status |
10	| `machine-config-controller` | Deployment | Runs render, template, node, kubelet-config, container-runtime-config, certrotation, drain controllers |
11	| `machine-config-daemon` | DaemonSet | Node agent: fetches and applies configs, handles reboot/drain |
12	| `machine-config-server` | DaemonSet (masters) | Serves Ignition configs to bootstrapping/joining nodes via HTTPS |
13	| `machine-os-builder` | Deployment | Orchestrates OCL builds (MachineOSBuild jobs) |
14	| `machine-config-osimagestream` | Job | Generates OSImageStream from release payload |
15	| `apiserver-watcher` | DaemonSet | Monitors local apiserver, writes cloud-routes downfiles |
16	
17	## Controller Details
18	
19	### Template Controller (`pkg/controller/template/`)
20	- **Watches:** ControllerConfig, APIServer, Secrets, ConfigMaps
21	- **Produces:** Base MachineConfig objects from Go templates in `/etc/mcc/templates`
22	- **Templates organized by role:** `common/`, `master/`, `worker/`
23	- Merges IRI (Internal Release Image) secrets into generated configs
24	- Template rendering uses Go `text/template` with cluster state as input
25	
26	### Render Controller (`pkg/controller/render/`)
27	- **Watches:** MachineConfig, MachineConfigPool, OSImageStream, ControllerConfig, ContainerRuntimeConfig
28	- **Produces:** Rendered (merged) MachineConfig per pool
29	- Merge is deterministic: MachineConfigs sorted by name, last-writer-wins for file conflicts
30	- Uses content-based hashing for rendered config naming (avoids unnecessary updates)
31	- 5-second render delay to batch rapid changes
32	
33	### Node Controller (`pkg/controller/node/`)
34	- **Watches:** MachineConfigPool, MachineConfig, MachineOSConfig, MachineOSBuild, Node
35	- **Manages:** Node annotations (desired/current config), drain coordination, MachineConfigNode status
36	- Largest controller (~81KB). Orchestrates the full node update lifecycle:
37	  1. Detects config drift between desired and current
38	  2. Respects `maxUnavailable` for rolling updates
39	  3. Coordinates with MCD for drain, apply, reboot
40	  4. Updates MachineConfigPool status counters
41	
42	### Build Controller (`pkg/controller/build/`)
43	- **Watches:** MachineOSBuild, MachineOSConfig, Job, MachineConfigPool
44	- **Manages:** OCL build lifecycle via Kubernetes Jobs
45	- Subdirectories: `buildrequest/` (build job creation), `imagebuilder/` (podman/buildah), `imagepruner/` (cleanup)
46	- Creates Jobs that run `podman build` with user-provided Containerfiles
47	- Tracks build status through MachineOSBuild conditions
48	
49	### KubeletConfig Controller (`pkg/controller/kubelet-config/`)
50	- **Watches:** KubeletConfig, MachineConfigPool, ClusterOperator
51	- Merges upstream KubeletConfiguration into MachineConfig objects
52	- Handles feature gate propagation to kubelet config
53	- Supports `autoSizingReserved` for automatic resource reservation
54	
55	### ContainerRuntimeConfig Controller (`pkg/controller/container-runtime-config/`)
56	- **Watches:** ContainerRuntimeConfig, MachineConfigPool
57	- Generates CRI-O config files (`/etc/crio/crio.conf.d/`), storage config, registries config
58	- Large helper surface (~63KB helpers.go) for registry mirror configuration
59	
60	### Boot Image Controller (`pkg/controller/bootimage/`)
61	- **Watches:** MachineSet, ControlPlaneMachineSet, Infrastructure, ClusterVersion
62	- Updates cloud provider boot images (AMIs, Azure images, GCP images)
63	- Platform-specific logic for AWS, Azure, GCP, vSphere, OpenStack
64	- AMI mapping in `ami.go` (228KB of region-to-AMI mappings)
65	
66	### Other Controllers
67	- **CertRotation** (`pkg/controller/certrotation/`): Rotates MCO serving certificates
68	- **PinnedImageSet** (`pkg/controller/pinnedimageset/`): Ensures images are pre-pulled to nodes
69	- **Drain** (`pkg/controller/drain/`): Coordinates node drain operations
70	- **InternalReleaseImage** (`pkg/controller/internalreleaseimage/`): Extracts image refs from release payloads
71	
72	## Machine Config Daemon (`pkg/daemon/`)
73	
74	The node-level agent. Core file `daemon.go` is ~110KB, `update.go` is ~135KB.
75	
76	**Responsibilities:**
77	- Watches node annotations for desired config changes
78	- Applies Ignition configs: writes files, creates systemd units, sets kernel arguments
79	- Manages OS updates via rpm-ostree (traditional) or bootc (container-native)
80	- Decides reboot vs. service-reload based on change type
81	- Config drift monitoring (detects unauthorized file changes)
82	- Reports progress via MachineConfigNode conditions
83	- Pinned image set management (pre-pulling images)
84	- On-disk validation of applied configs
85	
86	**Update decision tree:**
87	- File-only changes with no OS change: service reload (CRIO, kubelet)
88	- OS image change or kernel args: full node reboot
89	- FIPS change: reboot required
90	- Extension changes: reboot required
91	
92	## Machine Config Server (`pkg/server/`)
93	
94	HTTPS server on master nodes serving Ignition configs to joining nodes.
95	
96	- `AppendersBuilder` pattern composes config from multiple sources
97	- Appenders: KubeConfig, certificates, node annotations
98	- Bootstrap mode: serves from filesystem during cluster install
99	- Cluster mode: serves from rendered MachineConfigs via API
100	
101	## Operator Sync (`pkg/operator/`)
102	
103	Top-level orchestration in `sync.go` (~113KB):
104	
105	1. Syncs OSImageStream (image references)
106	2. Renders and deploys controller, daemon, server components
107	3. Manages RBAC, ServiceAccounts, ConfigMaps, Secrets
108	4. Reports ClusterOperator status and conditions
109	5. Handles upgrades: version skew, feature gate transitions
110	
111	## Shared Infrastructure
112	
113	### `pkg/controller/common/`
114	- `controller_context.go`: Shared informer factory, client sets, feature gate state
115	- `helpers.go` (~56KB): MC manipulation, Ignition utilities, annotation helpers
116	- `layered_node_state.go`: State tracking for OCL nodes
117	- `metrics.go`: Prometheus metric definitions
118	- `featuregates.go`: Feature gate detection and propagation
119	
120	### `lib/resourceapply/` and `lib/resourceread/`
121	- Idempotent resource application (create-or-update pattern)
122	- Type-safe resource deserialization from YAML/JSON
123	
124	## ValidatingAdmissionPolicies
125	
126	Located in `manifests/`:
127	- `custom-machine-config-pool-selector`: Custom MCPs must inherit from worker
128	- `internalreleaseimage-deletion-guard`: Protects InternalReleaseImage from deletion
129	- `machineconfigpool-osimagestream-reference`: OSImageStream ref validation
130	- `machineconfiguration-guards`: Global MachineConfiguration guards
131	- `mcn-guards`: MachineConfigNode guards
132	- `osimagestream-deletion-guard`: Protects OSImageStream from deletion
133	- `update-bootimages`: Boot image update validation
134

7	)
